title: eriosorus retrofractus
intro: Eriosorus retrofractus, also known as the Rock Tassel Fern or the Mountain Fern, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eriosorus retrofractus include reducing fever, soothing coughs, and relieving congestion, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various respiratory ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
